findings.issue: Async OAuth init handlers can call window.location.assign after the component has unmounted. LoginPage and InvitationPage await initOAuth inside a button click handler with no abort or mounted-state guard, so a pending Promise can resolve after the user navigates away and redirect the wrong page.
findings.remediation: Track the handler lifetime with a useRef-mounted flag or an AbortController created in the handler and aborted on cleanup; guard window.location.assign so it only runs while the component is still mounted.

findings.issue: The double-click guard reads the loading state from a React closure, so two rapid clicks can both see loading===false before the state update flushes. This can overwrite the stored PKCE verifier with a second value while the browser uses the first provider authorization URL, causing a verifier/challenge mismatch when the user returns.
findings.remediation: Replace the state-based guard with a ref-based lock that is synchronously toggled at the start of the handler (e.g., useRef(false) checked and set before any async work).

findings.issue: ImportMetaEnv declares VITE_API_BASE_URL and VITE_APP_BASE_URL as required strings, but every runtime call site uses an optional fallback (|| '' or || window.location.origin). The type contract hides the optional intent and allows future code to assume the variables are always set.
findings.remediation: Declare both env vars as optional: `readonly VITE_API_BASE_URL?: string` and `readonly VITE_APP_BASE_URL?: string` to match the documented .env.example and runtime fallback logic.
